texto: The greatest virtue consists in flourishing to the greatest of our capacities.
explicacao: Citação de Annabel Lyon sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “The greatest virtue consists in flourishing to the greatest of our capacities.” articula motivacao, inspiracional, sabedoria em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e sabedoria.

frase_original: The greatest virtue consists in flourishing to the greatest of our capacities.

texto: You who understand what a human mind can be, how can you bear it? I don't have the hundredth part of your mind and there are days when I think I'll go mad. I can feel it. Or hear it. It's more like hearing something creeping along the walls, just behind my head, getting closer and closer. A big insect, maybe a scorpion. A dry skittering, that's what madness sounds like to me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Annabel Lyon: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “You who understand what a human mind can be, how can you bear it? I don't have the hundredth part…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, madness, closer pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
